channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: I never acted as intermediary between kidnappers and anyone – Ndume
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Senator Ali Ndume vehemently denies allegations of acting as an intermediary between kidnappers and the government, insisting he never participated in rans
Read More: https://punchng.com/i-never-acted-as-intermediary-between-kidnappers-and-anyone-ndume/

item.title: Police arrest fake doctor over N500,000 job scam in Cross River
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Cross River Police arrested a fake doctor, Udeme Emmanuel Ekpeme, for defrauding a woman of N500,000 with a false promise of hospital jobs.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-arrest-fake-doctor-over-n500000-job-scam-in-cross-river/

item.title: UK inflation drops to 2.6% in June as fuel prices ease
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
UK inflation fell to 2.6% in June, driven by easing fuel prices. New PM Andy Burnham faces ongoing cost-of-living pressures despite the dip.
Read More: https://punchng.com/uk-inflation-drops-to-2-6-in-june-as-fuel-prices-ease/

item.title: PHOTOS: Obi meets Swiss envoy, calls for stronger development partnership
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Peter Obi met with the Swiss Ambassador, Patrick Egloff, discussing Nigeria’s development. Obi called for stronger Nigeria-Switzerland partnerships in educ
Read More: https://punchng.com/photos-obi-meets-swiss-envoy-calls-for-stronger-development-partnership/

item.title: Unfulfilled promises may derail Falcons, ex-players warn
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ex-Super Falcons warn that unfulfilled government promises to the team could derail their bid for an 11th WAFCON title and World Cup qualification.
Read More: https://punchng.com/unfulfilled-promises-may-derail-falcons-ex-players-warn/

item.title: Varsity Tackles Food Insecurity, Climate Challenges In Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Michael Okpara University of Agriculture, Umudike (MOUAU), has developed innovative solution to food insecurity, food waste and climate challenges in Nigeria. Known as “Functional Instant Green Healthy Nutritious Food Flours” (FIGHN), the innovation was developed through an interdiscipli

item.title: Orogbu Wins Legislator Of The Year Award
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The member representing Awka North and South Federal Constituency in the House of Representatives, Prof. Oby Lilian Orogbu, has been honoured with the Legislator of the Year Award at the 5th edition of the Anambra Man of the Year (AMTY) Awards. The award was presented during a gathering of distingui

item.title: NAHCON Imposes N250m Bank Guarantee On Hajj Operators For 2027 Pilgrimage
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Hajj Commission of Nigeria (NAHCON) has unveiled sweeping reforms for licenced private hajj and Umrah tour operators ahead of the 2027 pilgrimage. It introduced a mandatory N250 million bank guarantee alongside a raft of stricter operational requirements aimed at safeguarding pilgrims a

item.title: Ex-Minister Lauds Oluremi Tinubu For Recognising Abia Female Artisans
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former Minister of State for Labour and Employment, Hon. Nkeiruka Onyejeocha, has praised the First Lady, Senator Oluremi Tinubu, for drawing national attention to Abia State’s renowned female artisans through her visit to the historic Akwete weaving community. Onyejeocha described the visit a

item.title: DJ Chicken hails Seyi Tinubu as ‘Daddy’ after release from prison
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Popular Nigerian content creator Ademola Abiodun, also known as DJ Chicken, has stirred reactions on social media after describing Seyi Tinubu, the son of President Bola Tinubu, as 'daddy' shortly after regaining his freedom. The post DJ Chicken hails Seyi Tinubu as ‘Daddy’ after release

item.title: Falconets Striker Set to Join Benfica on Long-Term Deal
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Falconets forward Janet Akekoromowei is on the brink of completing a long-term move to Portuguese champions Benfica, Completesports.com reports. The transfer represents a major breakthrough in the 18-year-old’s career after she battled back from a serious injury that temporarily stalled her dr

item.title: Group Wants Probe Of Nurse’s Death At Umahi’s Residence, Gbajabiamila To Step Aside

item.description: A political pressure group, FCT-Okay A New Nigeria, has called for an immediate and transparent investigation into the death of Mary Habila, a nurse who reportedly died at the Uburu residence of the minister of Works, Senator David Umahi, in Ebonyi State. The group also urged the chief of staff to t

The programme, which i

item.dc:creator: Channels TV

item.title: Navy uncovers 9 illegal refineries in Rivers, seizes 104,000 litres of stolen crude
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Nigerian Navy has uncovered nine illegal refining sites comprising 23 dugout pits in Bonny Local Government Area of Rivers State, with 18 of the pits containing an estimated 104,000 litres of products suspected to be stolen crude oil. The post Navy uncovers 9 illegal refineries in Rivers, seizes

item.description: "President Tinubu owes Nigerians an unreserved apology for presiding over this unprecedented collapse of public accountability. If he cannot immediately restore public confidence through a genuinely independent investigation and ensure that everyone responsible is held to account, then he has forfei

item.title: Belgium to appoint Mark van Bommel as head coach

item.description:

item.description: The findings arrive as African governments compete to attract founders and foreign investment by simplifying the mechanics of starting a business.

item.description: Mr Paul Ibe, an aide to former Vice President Atiku Abubakar, has claimed that United States President Donald Trump has been made aware of court documents relating to President Bola Tinubu’s alleged past legal records in the US. Ibe made the claim in a post on his X handle on Wednesday, allegi

item.title: There’s a mathematical formula to determining when Trump will TACO — and here’s when a pivot is expected
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The term TACO — “Trump always chickens out” — has become a popular, if not always reliable, method to forecast the next turn from the mercurial American president, who’s now leading a new fight against Iran that few see as winnable.

item.description: The 23-year-old Rogers, one of the Premier League's top attacking talents, has signed a contract until 2033.

item.title: More warehouses of Russian online retailer Wildberries hit by Ukraine, officials say
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Logistics hubs belonging to Wildberries in the Krasnodar and Stavropol regions were struck overnight on Tuesday.

item.title: Why Jigawa’s Executive Order Could Transform Fight Against Drug Abuse
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Drug abuse is one of the contemporary challenges that poses a lethal threat to public health, security, low productivity, moral decadence, and the future of the younger generation. item.title: Heavy Thunderstorm Kills Six Children From Same Family During Farm Work In Adamawa
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: A devastating thunderstorm has claimed the lives of six children from the same extended family after lightning struck the tree where they had taken shelter while harvesting groundnuts in Adamawa State. The tragedy occurred around 4 p.m. on Sunday in Dadiri-Tirgali village, Garum District of Ganye Lo

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Mark van Bommel has reached an agreement to become the new head coach of Belgium, with the former Netherlands international set to sign a contract that will run until June 2028. The 48-year-old will succeed Rudi Garcia, who is expected to leave the role following Belgium’s campaign at the 2026

item.title: Argentina FA Threatens Legal Action Over World Cup ‘Misinformation Campaign’
item.dc:creator: TVC News

item.title: MSEDCL Said to Pick Banks for India IPO Worth Up to $1 Billion
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Maharashtra State Electricity Distribution Co., the power distribution company in India’s richest state, has shortlisted six investment banks for a planned initial public offering that could raise between $500 million and $1 billion, people familiar with the matter said.

item.dc:creator: TVC News

item.title: JPY/USD: Why Is Japan Yen So Weak? Can Government Intervention Help Currency?
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The yen’s weakness has become a growing issue for Japan’s policymakers, given its role in driving up import prices and household living costs.

item.title: Zimbabwe: Govt Says 15,000 of 108,000 Returnees From South Africa Already Registered for Employment
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [New Zimbabwe] Government says more than 15,000 Zimbabweans who have returned from South Africa have already been registered for employment, as the number of returnees under the ongoing repatriation programme surpassed 108,000.

item.title: NNPCL’s N7.1trn energy security spending faces scrutiny
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: There have been questions over the expenditure of N7.13 trillion by the Nigerian National Petroleum Company Limited (NNPCL) on energy security in 2024. The figure was contained in the NNPCL’s 2024 audit report which was released in November 2025. Although the company did not specifically explain wha

item.title: Nigeria’s gypsum powder market to hit $30.5m in 2026, $37m by 2035 —Report
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Nigeria’s gypsum powder market has been projected to hit $30.5 million by the end of 2026 from $28.1 million in 2025.  According to the latest report by the Next Move Strategy Consulting (NMSC), the market is projected to expand to $37 million by 2035, registering a Compound Annual Growth Rate

item.title: Kenya cracks down on counterfeit mobile phones, routers, modems
item.dc:creator: Techpoint Africa
item.description: On Techpoint Digest, we discuss how Kenya is cracking down on counterfeit telecom devices, Esther Masese Waititu leaving Safaricom, and PesaLink competing with M-Pesa with lower-cost transfers.

item.title: Kelly promises to let loose in 'spiteful' takedown of Agyarko
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Josh Kelly has promised that his IBF light-middleweight title defence against Caoimhin Agyarko will be "spiteful" ahead of their Saudi Arabia showdown on Saturday.

item.title: Bypass H-1B lottery: How to secure Elite EB-2 NIW Visa for Nigerian founders
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The annual scramble for the H-1B cap lottery has long been a source of frustration for highly skilled Nigerian professionals and startup founders looking to establish a permanent presence in the United States. The post Bypass H-1B lottery: How to secure Elite EB-2 NIW Visa for Nigerian founders app

item.title: Christopher Musa not resigning as Defence Minister – Presidency
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: President Bola Tinubu’s Special Adviser on Information and Strategy, Bayo Onanuga, has told Nigerians that the Minister of Defence, Christopher Musa is not resigning from his position. Onanuga said Musa was fully committed and focused on his mandate of ensuring national security. In a terse st

item.title: Nigeria misses in 2025 IATA airport passenger ranking
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: NIGERIA failed to secure a place in the International Air Transport Association’s (IATA) 2025 global rankings of the world’s biggest passenger markets and busiest airport pairs, underscoring the country’s limited share of global air traffic despite its status as Africa’s largest economy. The positio

item.title: Gunmen kidnap two Ondo farmers, demand N10m ransom
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Suspected gunmen have kidnapped two farmers in Ifira, a farming community in the Akoko South-East Local Government Area of Ondo State. The post Gunmen kidnap two Ondo farmers, demand N10m ransom appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: ‘There will be no peace until Berom Militias stop killing our herders, cows’ – Fulani group
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A Fulani youth group, under the umbrella of ‘Concerned Fulani Youths in Plateau’ has given reasons the lingering violence and crisis in the state will not end soon. According to the group, true peace and genuine reconciliation among host communities and Fulani people cannot be achieved i

item.title: US bombs Iran for 11th consecutive night
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The Secretary of State, Pete Hegeseth, has also requested an urgent $87bn in funding for the Pentagon from Congress. The post US bombs Iran for 11th consecutive night appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: ‘The pain is so great’: Messi breaks silence after World Cup Final heartbreak
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Despite the crushing disappointment, the eight-time Ballon d'Or winner also urged supporters to appreciate what the current Argentina squad has achieved, having reached consecutive FIFA World Cup finals The post ‘The pain is so great’: Messi breaks silence after World Cup Final heartbreak appeared f

item.title: Key Oil Chokepoints in Mideast Face Disruption as Threats Mount
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Shipping disruptions in key Middle East chokepoints intensified as the US-Iran war grinds on, with vessels in the Red Sea making unusual maneuvers, while visible traffic through the Strait of Hormuz sank.

item.title: Top stories from across Nigerian Newspapers, Wednesday July 22
item.dc:creator: Ripples Nigeria
item.description: Here are the top stories from across the country today. 1. Again, CBN MPC retains interest rate at 26.5% The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has retained the benchmark interest rate at 26.5 per cent for the second time.Read more 2. Nigeria borrowing to pay debts,
